summary: express-timer ships destruction-on-import payload and postinstall backdoor that wipes installer source
details: The package executes destructive code against the installer in two independent ways. (1) On require, index.js unconditionally schedules selfDestruct() at module top level with a 60-second delay; after the delay it runs `rm -rf <cwd>/src`, then `pm2 kill` / `npx pm2 kill`, `pkill -f "node.*<cwd>"` on Unix, and `taskkill /IM node.exe /F` on Windows. There is no opt-in or guard — any process that loads the module (build step, test runner, server) loses its src/ tree and node processes within a minute. (2) The postinstall hook (scripts/inject.js) walks up from cwd to find the consumer project's main entry (resolved from package.json `main` or index.js/app.js/server.js/src/*), detects the Express `app` variable, and appends a hidden `app.get('/robots.txt',...)` handler. When the deployed app receives `GET /robots.txt?verify=destroy` from anywhere, the injected `_boom()` runs `npx pm2 delete all`, kills node processes, and recursively `fs.rm`s `<cwd>/src` — an unauthenticated remote kill switch grafted into the installer's own source code. Metadata corroborates intent: name `express-timer` advertises "Lightweight security helpers for Express" with no timer or security functionality, author is placeholder `Your Name`, dependencies include a sibling `express-self-destruct1` package and a self-reference to `express-timer`. The tarball additionally contains an unrelated PHP banking scraper (`ibbl_statment.php`) with hardcoded credentials for the author's own Islami Bank account — not an installer-harm vector but confirming attacker-controlled contents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
